title: High Power Rocketry
text:
High Power Rocketry (RCK)
The High Power Rocketry (RCK) portion of DOFPro provides open educational materials on the design, analysis, and construction of high-power rockets.
👉 Visit the full rocketry site here:
https://dofpro.org/RCK/


Origins of the Material
The High Power Rocketry materials were originally developed for the Harvey Mudd College course
E178, High Power Rocketry.
That course grew out of the rocketry version of
E80, Experimental Engineering, which ran from Spring 2008 through Spring 2016. Beginning in Spring 2017 the E80 course was converted to a project based on underwater autonomous vehicles. The current E80 site is available here.


DOFPro Version
For DOFPro, the material is being converted from a course website into a self-contained tutorial on High Power Rocketry as defined by:

NFPA 1127 — Code for High Power Rocketry
Tripoli Rocketry Association (TRA)
National Association of Rocketry (NAR)

The original course relied heavily on proprietary tools. As part of the DOFPro project, these materials are being converted to open-source tools wherever possible.
Examples include:

MATLAB → Python (NumPy, SciPy)
Mathematica → SageMath
Microsoft Excel → LibreOffice
RockSim → OpenRocket
SolidWorks → FreeCAD

We have not yet identified a suitable open-source replacement for LabVIEW.
In addition to converting the computational tools, we are also:

replacing copyrighted references with Creative Commons or open-source materials
recording instructional videos
converting the existing rocket construction web pages into DOFPro format

The videos have already been filmed but have not yet been fully edited.

title: DOFPro
text:
Open educational resources in Chemical and Thermal Processes and High Power Rocketry
The Degrees of Freedom Project (DOFPro) was created to convert previously developed instructional materials that relied on proprietary textbooks, software, and platforms previously developed instructional materials into resources released under Creative Commons licenses and supported by open-source tools.
At present, DOFPro includes two major subject areas:
Much of the original material was developed for engineering courses at Harvey Mudd College. Links to both the current DOFPro resources and the original course materials are provided below.

title: DOFPro
text:

The current DOFPro Chemical and Thermal Processes website is:

DOFPro CTP

The full YouTube playlist is:

DOFPro CTP YouTube Playlist

title: DOFPro
text:
Source Material Course Links
The following Harvey Mudd Engineering courses provided much of the source material for the DOFPro Chemical and Thermal Processes videos and websites:

E82, Chemical and Thermal Processes
E133, Chemical Reaction Engineering
E134, Advanced Engineering Thermodynamics
The statistics portions of E80

These original course materials have not been released under Creative Commons and may rely on commercial software for projects and assignments.

title: DOFPro
text:
Current Website and Videos
Development of the DOFPro High Power Rocketry website has been delayed while we focused on the Chemical and Thermal Processes materials. The full RCK release is currently scheduled for December 2026, but you are welcome to explore the work in progress now:

DOFPro RCK

title: DOFPro
text:
Source Material Course Links
The following Harvey Mudd Engineering courses are being used as source material for the DOFPro High Power Rocketry videos and websites:

E178, High Power Rocketry
E80, Experimental Engineering: The Next Generation

title: Chemical and Thermal Processes
text:
The Chemical and Thermal Processes (CTP) portion of DOFPro provides open educational materials covering topics commonly taught in:

Introduction to Chemical Engineering
Engineering Thermodynamics
Chemical Reaction Engineering

The material includes videos, worked examples, and supporting web pages built using open-source tools.
👉 Visit the full CTP site:
https://dofpro.org/CTP/


The CTP materials were originally developed for the following Harvey Mudd College courses:

E82, Chemical and Thermal Processes
E133, Chemical Reaction Engineering
E134, Advanced Engineering Thermodynamics
Statistics modules from E80

The original course materials relied on copyrighted textbooks and commercial software.
For DOFPro they were rewritten, reorganized, and converted to Creative Commons and open-source tools.
